title: 404 error when accessing production webhook URL on Railway
symptom: Users receive a 404 error when trying to access the production webhook URL on Railway, while the test webhook URL works fine.
not_this_if: The error occurs on the test webhook URL
root_cause: The Railway server is configured with a separate instance for webhook processing, which results in a different URL structure for production webhooks compared to test webhooks. This misconfiguration leads to 404 errors when the incorrect URL is used.
fix_steps: Identify the correct production webhook URL format for Railway
Update the webhook URL in your n8n workflow to match the correct format
Test the updated webhook URL to ensure it responds correctly
agent_instruction: Update the production webhook URL in your n8n workflow to the correct format for Railway.
